title: Syrian Buttermilk Pilaf
description: A traditional Syrian dish featuring pillowy soft grains cooked in buttermilk, perfect for a comforting meal. This recipe is slow-cooked to bring out the rich flavors of the ingredients.
instructions: Rinse the basmati rice in a fine mesh sieve until the water runs clear, then soak it in water for 30 minutes.
Heat the ghee or oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to brown.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the cumin seeds and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Drain the soaked rice and add it to the saucepan, stirring to coat the rice with the oil and mix with the onion and spices.
Add the buttermilk, salt, and pepper to the saucepan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in the chopped parsley.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
